---
title: "Try the sample data"
---

ACE includes a guide-ready version of the Manchester Folk-Methods example dataset.

Use it to practise importing sources, importing a codebook, coding text, and exporting results.

## What is included

The sample folder contains:

- `sources.csv`: all 19 interview transcripts in CSV form.
- `sources/`: the same 19 transcripts as text files.
- `codebook.csv`: 132 codes converted from the deposited codebook PDF.

The CSV and folder routes contain the same source material. Use one route, not both.

## Get the files

Download or browse the sample package from the ACE repository:

[Open the sample data folder](https://github.com/usyd-soles-edu/annotation-coding-environment/tree/main/examples/ace-guide-manchester-folk-methods)

Keep the three parts together if you want to follow the guide exactly.

## Option 1: CSV import

1. Open ACE and create a new project.
2. Choose **Import sources**.
3. Select `sources.csv`.
4. Choose `source_id` as the source ID column.
5. Choose `text` as the text column.
6. Check the import preview.

ACE will create 19 sources named `P01` through `P19`.

## Option 2: Folder import

1. Open ACE and create a new project.
2. Choose **Import sources**.
3. Select the `sources/` folder.
4. Check the import preview.

![](assets/guide/folder-import-review.png)

ACE will import each `.txt` file as one source.

## Import the codebook

After importing sources:

1. Open the codebook menu.
2. Import `codebook.csv`.
3. Check that folders and codes appear in the codebook.

The sample codebook has:

- `name`: the code name
- `group`: the folder name
- `definition`: the code definition

ACE creates folders from the `group` column and codes from the `name` column.

For a codebook without folders, use a CSV with a `name` column and leave the folder mapping blank during import.

## Attribution

The sample is derived from:

Fflur, Myfanwy (2026). *Identifying "Folk-Methods" Employed With Fitness Applications By Users With Chronic Mobility Issues To Improve Application Design - Survey Results, Interview Transcripts and Codebook*. University of Manchester. Dataset. <https://doi.org/10.48420/31900453.v1>

Original dataset licence: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.
